title: schima wallichii var. superba
intro: Schima wallichii var. superba, also known as the "Coccia" or "Bengal oak," is a variant of the Schima wallichii plant species, which is native to the Himalayan region and other parts of Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain and swelling, and supporting respiratory and digestive health. In herbal preparations, Schima wallichii var. superba is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions to harness its medicinal properties.
